segments.title: Unsegmented transcript due to webhook timing issue
segments.summary: Peter reports an unsegmented transcript caused by an out-of-order webhook and KV write delays. To fix this, Justin proposes buffering segments in memory and implementing a 'soft close' on conversations to allow late-arriving segments to be appended.
actions: Justin: Add a short FIFO buffer and a 'soft close' on conversations for segmenting.
decisions: Buffer segments in memory for 60 seconds before committing.
segments.retrieval_summary: Fix unsegmented transcripts from webhook timing by buffering segments in memory and adding a 'soft close' to conversations; bug, kv_store.

segments.title: Generating test transcripts for segmentation simulation
segments.summary: In a thread, Amy offers to generate test transcripts to simulate the overlapping segment issue, and Peter requests that they include realistic filler words.
actions: Amy: Generate two short test transcripts to simulate overlap.
segments.retrieval_summary: Amy to generate test transcripts with realistic filler to simulate segmentation overlap for testing.

segments.title: Bug report: duplicate AI prompts on rapid submission
segments.summary: Peter reports a bug where submitting AI prompts too quickly creates duplicates. Justin identifies the cause as a dual client-side event firing and proposes a single dispatch path and a server-side idempotency key for a fix. Peter also requests logging the prompt UUID.
actions: Justin: Unify to a single dispatch and add an idempotency key server-side.
Peter: Please also log the prompt UUID at the edge for tracing.
segments.retrieval_summary: Fix duplicate AI prompts with server-side idempotency key and single dispatch path; log prompt uuid for tracing; bug-report, duplicates.

segments.title: Fix for duplicate AI prompts pushed and tested
segments.summary: Justin announces a fix for the duplicate prompt issue, which involves removing client-side debounce and adding server-side duplicate rejection. Peter confirms the fix is stable.
decisions: Implement server-side duplicate prompt rejection within a 30-second window.
segments.retrieval_summary: Fix duplicate AI prompts by implementing server-side rejection within a 30-second window; bug-fix, idempotency.

segments.title: Review of buffered segmentation improvements and edge cases
segments.summary: Amy notes that the new buffered segmentation has improved diarization but points out an edge case with silent gaps over five minutes. Justin clarifies this is intended behavior, and Peter agrees to keep it for predictability.
decisions: Keep the five-minute silent gap as a conversation splitter for now.
segments.retrieval_summary: Maintain five-minute silent gap as a conversation splitter in buffered segmentation for predictability; diarization, edge-case.
